content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a browser-based Yew/Rust/WASM p-code VM debugger for pv24a running on COR24 emulator. Two-level debugging: p-code semantic level (primary) and COR24 host drill-down (secondary). Phase 1: scaffold Yew project with Trunk, wire cor24-emulator. Phase 2: control bar + p-code disassembly panel. Phase 3: eval stack + call frame panels. Phase 4: memory region map. Phase 5: host/COR24 drill-down panel. Phase 6: delta highlighting and step-over/step-out. Phase 7: demo registry with embedded .spc test programs.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-scaffold-yew-project [legacy]: Scaffold the Yew/Rust/WASM project. Create Cargo.toml (edition 2024, Yew 0.21...
  [x] 002-002-disassembly-panel [production]: Add COR24 disassembly panel and improve the debugger layout. Since p-code VM ...
  [x] 003-003-call-frame-memory [production]: Add call frame panel and memory region map to the p-code VM debugger. Specifi...
  [ ] 004-004-memory-hex-viewer [legacy]: Add a hex dump memory viewer panel. Allow selecting which memory region to in... <-- YOU ARE HERE

=== CURRENT STEP: 004-004-memory-hex-viewer [legacy] ===
Status: pending
Description: Add a hex dump memory viewer panel. Allow selecting which memory region to in...
Task type: yew-component

=== PROMPT ===
Add a hex dump memory viewer panel. Allow selecting which memory region to inspect (code, eval stack, call stack, globals, heap) via dropdown. Show hex bytes with ASCII sidebar, 16 bytes per row, with address column. Highlight the current PC position in the code region and stack pointers in their respective regions. Also add step-over and step-out p-code stepping modes: step-over runs until the call stack depth returns to current level, step-out runs until call stack depth decreases by one.

=== SKILL: yew-component (v1) ===
Create a Yew component for browser-based UI

Procedure:
  1. Define the component struct with Properties if needed
  2. Implement the Component trait (create, update, view)
  3. Write the html! macro template for rendering
  4. Add message types for user interactions
  5. Wire up callbacks in the view
  6. Build with trunk serve to test in browser

Known failure modes:
  - missing_trunk (2x): Forgetting to install or configure trunk for WASM building
  - stale_state (3x): Not cloning state handle before moving into closure

=== PAST SUCCESSES (yew-component) ===
  [2026-03-25T21:36:08] action=Read pvm.s call/enter/leave/ret to understand 12-byte frame layout. Walked fp_vm-12 chain for dynamic link traversal. Used web-tf24a memory_regions pattern for proportional bar. Added prev_eval_stack/prev_pcode_state for delta highlighting. Playwright for UI verification., result=success, reward=+1
  [2026-03-25T21:06:44] action=Read pvm.s to understand VM state struct layout and fetch-decode-execute cycle. Used emulator.read_word() to read vm_state fields. Used emulator.disassemble() for host panel. Built p-code decoder from dispatch table opcodes. Used vm_loop label address for step boundary detection. Playwright for UI testing., result=success, reward=+1

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

content.content:
1→//! P-code VM debugger component — two-level debugging with p-code semantic
     2→//! layer (primary) and COR24 host implementation layer (secondary drill-down).
     3→
     4→use crate::config::VmConfig;
     5→use cor24_emulator::{Assembler, EmulatorCore, StopReason};
     6→use gloo::timers::callback::Timeout;
     7→use std::collections::HashMap;
     8→use yew::prelude::*;
     9→
    10→/// Execution batch size per tick (instructions).
    11→const BATCH_SIZE: u64 = 50_000;
    12→
    13→/// Tick interval in milliseconds.
    14→const TICK_MS: u32 = 25;
    15→
    16→/// Number of COR24 disassembly lines to show before current PC.
    17→const DISASM_BEFORE: usize = 8;
    18→
    19→/// Number of COR24 disassembly lines to show after current PC.
    20→const DISASM_AFTER: usize = 16;
    21→
    22→/// P-code opcode names indexed by opcode number.
    23→const PCODE_NAMES: &[(u8, &str)] = &[
    24→    (0x00, "halt"),
    25→    (0x01, "push"),
    26→    (0x02, "push_s"),
    27→    (0x03, "dup"),
    28→    (0x04, "drop"),
    29→    (0x05, "swap"),
    30→    (0x06, "over"),
    31→    (0x10, "add"),
    32→    (0x11, "sub"),
    33→    (0x12, "mul"),
    34→    (0x13, "div"),
    35→    (0x14, "mod"),
    36→    (0x15, "neg"),
    37→    (0x16, "and"),
    38→    (0x17, "or"),
    39→    (0x18, "xor"),
    40→    (0x19, "not"),
    41→    (0x1A, "shl"),
    42→    (0x1B, "shr"),
    43→    (0x20, "eq"),
    44→    (0x21, "ne"),
    45→    (0x22, "lt"),
    46→    (0x23, "le"),
    47→    (0x24, "gt"),
    48→    (0x25, "ge"),
    49→    (0x30, "jmp"),
    50→    (0x31, "jz"),
    51→    (0x32, "jnz"),
    52→    (0x33, "call"),
    53→    (0x34, "ret"),
    54→    (0x35, "calln"),
    55→    (0x36, "trap"),
    56→    (0x40, "enter"),
    57→    (0x41, "leave"),
    58→    (0x42, "loadl"),
    59→    (0x43, "storel"),
    60→    (0x44, "loadg"),
    61→    (0x45, "storeg"),
    62→    (0x46, "addrl"),
    63→    (0x47, "addrg"),
    64→    (0x48, "loada"),
    65→    (0x49, "storea"),
    66→    (0x4A, "loadn"),
    67→    (0x4B, "storen"),
    68→    (0x50, "load"),
    69→    (0x51, "store"),
    70→    (0x52, "loadb"),
    71→    (0x53, "storeb"),
    72→    (0x60, "sys"),
    73→];
    74→
    75→/// vm_state struct field offsets (each field is a 24-bit word = 3 bytes).
    76→mod vm_offsets {
    77→    pub const PC: u32 = 0;
    78→    pub const ESP: u32 = 3;
    79→    pub const CSP: u32 = 6;
    80→    pub const FP_VM: u32 = 9;
    81→    pub const GP: u32 = 12;
    82→    pub const HP: u32 = 15;
    83→    pub const CODE: u32 = 18;
    84→    pub const STATUS: u32 = 21;
    85→    pub const TRAP_CODE: u32 = 24;
    86→}
    87→
    88→/// Decoded p-code instruction.
    89→struct PcodeInstr {
    90→    addr: u32,
    91→    name: &'static str,
    92→    operand: Option<u32>,
    93→    size: u32,
    94→}
    95→
    96→/// A decoded call frame from the call stack.
    97→struct CallFrame {
    98→    /// Return p-code PC.
    99→    return_pc: u32,
   100→    /// Dynamic link (caller's fp_vm).
   101→    dynamic_link: u32,
   102→    /// Static link (for lexical scoping).
   103→    static_link: u32,
   104→    /// Saved eval stack pointer at call time.
   105→    saved_esp: u32,
   106→}
   107→
   108→/// A named memory region for the region map.
   109→struct MemRegion {
   110→    name: &'static str,
   111→    css_class: &'static str,
   112→    start: u32,
   113→    size: u32,
   114→}
   115→
   116→/// Snapshot of the p-code VM's semantic state (read from emulator memory).
   117→#[derive(Clone)]
   118→struct PcodeState {
   119→    pc: u32,
   120→    esp: u32,
   121→    csp: u32,
   122→    fp_vm: u32,
   123→    gp: u32,
   124→    hp: u32,
   125→    code_base: u32,
   126→    status: u32,
   127→    trap_code: u32,
   128→}
   129→
   130→/// Messages driving the debugger state machine.
   131→pub enum Msg {
   132→    /// Load VM assembly and initialize the emulator.
   133→    Init,
   134→    /// Run a batch of instructions.
   135→    Tick,
   136→    /// Reset emulator to initial state.
   137→    Reset,
   138→    /// Step one p-code instruction (run COR24 until VM pc changes).
   139→    StepPcode,
   140→    /// Step one COR24 host instruction.
   141→    StepHost,
   142→    /// Toggle run/pause.
   143→    PauseResume,
   144→    /// Toggle COR24 drill-down panel visibility.
   145→    ToggleHost,
   146→}
   147→
   148→pub struct Debugger {
   149→    emulator: EmulatorCore,
   150→    config: VmConfig,
   151→    output: String,
   152→    running: bool,
   153→    halted: bool,
   154→    _tick_handle: Option<Timeout>,
   155→    prev_regs: [u32; 8],
   156→    prev_pc: u32,
   157→    labels: HashMap<String, u32>,
   158→    reverse_labels: HashMap<u32, String>,
   159→    program_end: u32,
   160→    /// Address of vm_state struct in emulator memory.
   161→    vm_state_addr: u32,
   162→    /// Address of eval_stack base.
   163→    eval_stack_base: u32,
   164→    /// Address of call_stack base.
   165→    call_stack_base: u32,
   166→    /// Address of code_seg.
   167→    code_seg_addr: u32,
   168→    /// Address of vm_loop (top of fetch-decode-execute cycle).
   169→    vm_loop_addr: u32,
   170→    /// Previous p-code PC for change detection.
   171→    prev_pcode_pc: u32,
   172→    /// Previous eval stack for change highlighting.
   173→    prev_eval_stack: Vec<u32>,
   174→    /// Previous VM state for change highlighting.
   175→    prev_pcode_state: Option<PcodeState>,
   176→    /// Total COR24 instructions executed.
   177→    instruction_count: u64,
   178→    /// Show COR24 host drill-down panel.
   179→    show_host: bool,
   180→}
   181→
   182→impl Debugger {
   183→    fn load_binary(&mut self) {
   184→        let mut asm = Assembler::new();
   185→        let result = asm.assemble(self.config.assembly());
   186→
   187→        if !result.errors.is_empty() {
   188→            self.output = "Assembly errors:\n".to_string();
   189→            for e in &result.errors {
   190→                self.output.push_str(e);
   191→                self.output.push('\n');
   192→            }
   193→            return;
   194→        }
   195→
   196→        self.labels = result.labels.clone();
   197→        self.reverse_labels = result
   198→            .labels
   199→            .iter()
   200→            .map(|(name, &addr)| (addr, name.clone()))

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add hex memory viewer panel and step-over/step-out modes

Adds a collapsible hex dump memory viewer with region selection dropdown
(code, eval stack, call stack, globals, heap), 16 bytes per row with
address column and ASCII sidebar, and PC/pointer highlighting. Also adds
step-over (runs until call depth returns to current level) and step-out
(runs until call depth decreases by one) p-code stepping modes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit hex viewer and step modes
